the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party

actor_claims.actor_label: any person that has made, or is considering making an alternative proposal
actor_claims.quote_text: any person that has made, or is considering making an alternative proposal

event_claims.description: The merger agreement restricts the Company, its affiliates and representatives from soliciting, initiating, encouraging or facilitating alternative proposals or related inquiries, discussions or offers.

The merger agreement restricts the Company, its affiliates and representatives from participating in discussions or negotiations or furnishing nonpublic information regarding alternative proposals, except to notify persons of the non-solicitation provisions.
event_claims.quote_text: participate in any discussions or negotiations regarding an alternative proposal with, or furnish any nonpublic information regarding the Company or its subsidiaries in connection with an alternative proposal to, any person that has made, or is considering making an alternative proposal (except to notify such person as to the non-solicitation provisions of the merger agreement);

event_claims.description: The merger agreement restricts the Company, its affiliates and representatives from entering into agreements relating to alternative proposals, except permitted confidentiality agreements.
event_claims.quote_text: enter into any letter of intent, agreement in principle, merger or acquisition agreement or any other agreement relating to or providing for any alternative proposal (except for confidentiality agreements permitted under the merger agreement).

event_claims.description: Before stockholder approval, if the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party that the board judges to constitute or reasonably be expected to result in a superior proposal, the Company may provide nonpublic information after entering into an acceptable confidentiality agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: provide nonpublic information to the third party after entering into a confidentiality agreement acceptable under the terms of the merger agreement;

event_claims.description: Before stockholder approval, if the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party that the board judges to constitute or reasonably be expected to result in a superior proposal, the Company may engage in discussions or negotiations with that third party.
event_claims.quote_text: engage in discussions or negotiations with a third party with respect to the alternative proposal.

event_claims.description: To change its recommendation or terminate the merger agreement for a superior proposal, the Company must give Parent written notice of the board's intention and the terms of the superior proposal.
event_claims.quote_text: provide Parent written notice of the board’s intention to make a change in recommendation or to terminate the merger agreement with a description of the terms of the superior proposal;

event_claims.description: To change its recommendation or terminate the merger agreement for a superior proposal, the Company must give Parent its reasons in writing.
event_claims.quote_text: provide to Parent in writing its reasons for making the change in recommendation or for terminating the merger agreement;

event_claims.description: Parent receives a three-business-day matching period to propose changes to the merger agreement, with extensions for material changes in the superior proposal, during which the Company must negotiate in good faith.
event_claims.quote_text: provide Parent with three business days to propose changes to the terms of the merger agreement (which will be extended for additional two business day periods for any material change in the superior proposal), and during this period negotiate with Parent in good faith to make adjustments to the terms and conditions of the merger agreement.

event_claims.description: After the Parent matching period, the board must determine that the superior proposal remains superior to the merger proposal after considering Parent's proposed revisions.
event_claims.quote_text: Following the end of such three business day period (and any extension thereto), after taking into account any revisions to the terms and conditions of the merger agreement proposed by Parent, the board must determine that the superior proposal remains superior to the merger proposal.

event_claims.description: The Company may terminate the merger agreement before the Company stockholder meeting to accept a superior proposal if it pays Parent a $255 million termination fee and promptly enters into a definitive agreement for the superior proposal.
event_claims.quote_text:
at any time prior to the Company stockholder meeting to accept a “superior proposal” (defined below under The Merger Agreement — Other Covenants and Agreements — No Solicitation ) so

long as (i) prior to or substantially concurrent with such termination, the Company has paid a termination fee of $255 million to Parent and (ii) promptly after such termination, the Company enters into a definitive agreement with respect to such superior proposal;

event_claims.description: Parent may terminate the merger agreement if the board makes a change of recommendation before the stockholder meeting.
event_claims.quote_text: if the board takes an action that constitutes a change of recommendation (as defined below under The Merger Agreement — Other Covenants and Agreements — No Solicitation ) prior to the stockholder meeting to adopt the merger agreement;

event_claims.description: The Company will owe Parent a $255 million termination fee if it terminates before stockholder approval in response to a superior proposal and promptly enters into a definitive agreement for that proposal.
event_claims.quote_text: the Company has terminated the merger agreement before stockholder approval of the merger proposal is obtained in response to a superior proposal and promptly after such termination the Company enters into a definitive agreement with respect to such superior proposal;

event_claims.description: The Company will owe Parent a $255 million termination fee if Parent terminates the merger agreement after a change of recommendation.
event_claims.quote_text: Parent has terminated the merger agreement in the event of a change of recommendation;

event_claims.description: The Company will owe Parent a $255 million termination fee if the merger is terminated under specified conditions and the Company enters into or completes a qualifying transaction within 12 months.
event_claims.quote_text: the merger is terminated under specified conditions and concurrently with or within 12 months after such termination, the Company enters into a definitive agreement providing for a qualifying transaction (as defined below under “ *The Merger Agreement — Termination Fees* ”) or completes a qualifying transaction.

event_claims.description: The Company entered into the merger agreement on December 14, 2014.
event_claims.quote_text: On December 14, 2014, the Company entered into the merger agreement providing for the merger of Merger Sub, a wholly owned subsidiary of Parent, with and into the Company, with the Company surviving the merger as a wholly owned subsidiary of Parent.
